title: [FIRRTL] Make width inference lazy
body:
The `InferWidths` pass on the FIRRTL dialect greedily constructs a constraint expression tree as it traverses the IR. There already is an optimization which at least lets it skip a module if it contains no uninferred widths at all. However in many cases there are just a handful of widths to be inferred, such that only a small portion of the generated constraint expressions end up being used.

Rather than having such a binary all-or-nothing approach, the `InferWidths` pass could operate lazily by walking a module's IR, and upon encountering an operation that imposes a constraint (`ConnectOp`, `PartialConnectOp`, `Attach`), recursively generate the constraint expression for its operands and memoize the result. This would do expression construction and detection of skippable modules in one go, and would make the pre-pass that ensures modules have constraint variables declared for their ports obsolete.
